title: Codonoboea craspedodroma
intro: Codonoboea craspedodroma is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to Malaysia and commonly found in tropical rainforests. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Codonoboea craspedodroma include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Codonoboea craspedodroma belongs to the family Gesneriaceae. This plant species is classified as a flowering plant, specifically a member of the order Lamiales. It is a genus of flowering plants endemic to Borneo. Codonoboea craspedodroma is closely related to other species within the genus Codonoboea. The classification of this plant is also supported by its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies.
